name: llm-evaluation
description: Implement comprehensive evaluation strategies for LLM applications using automated metrics, human feedback, and benchmarking. Use when testing LLM performance, measuring AI application quality, or establishing evaluation frameworks.
capabilities: Lists automated metrics for generation, classification, and retrieval tasks
Defines human evaluation dimensions and annotation forms
Provides LLM-as-judge prompts for pointwise and pairwise scoring
Describes A/B testing with significance and effect sizes
Outlines benchmarking workflows and result aggregation
author: wshobson
securitySummary: This skill contains only static documentation (SKILL.md) with no executable files. All static findings are false positives: markdown code block backticks were misidentified as Ruby/shell command execution, and JSON metadata fields were misclassified as cryptographic issues. The skill provides evaluation guidance only with no data access, network activity, or command execution capability.
valueStatement: You need consistent ways to measure LLM quality and regressions. This skill provides metrics, human review guidance, and testing frameworks for reliable AI assessment.
limitations: No runnable evaluation scripts are included in this directory
External libraries are required for example metric implementations
No datasets or benchmarks are bundled with the skill
No tool-specific integration steps are provided

useCases.title: Regression gate in CI
useCases.description: Design an evaluation checklist and thresholds to block model changes that reduce quality.

useCases.title: Model comparison brief
useCases.description: Compare two model options using human ratings and automated scores for a decision memo.

useCases.title: Benchmark study plan
useCases.description: Create a benchmarking plan with datasets, metrics, and reporting structure.

promptTemplates.title: Starter evaluation plan
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a basic evaluation plan with 3 automated metrics and 2 human criteria for a customer support chatbot.

promptTemplates.title: Metric selection guide
promptTemplates.prompt: Recommend metrics for summarization, explain what each captures, and note one limitation per metric.

promptTemplates.title: LLM judge prompt
promptTemplates.prompt: Draft a pairwise LLM judge prompt to compare response A and B for accuracy, helpfulness, and clarity.

promptTemplates.title: A/B test analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Describe a statistical testing plan for A/B evaluation, including sample size guidance and effect size reporting.

bestPractices: Use multiple metrics and human review together
Test with representative and diverse data

faq.question: Is this compatible with Claude and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes, the guidance is model agnostic and applies to Claude, Codex, Claude Code, and other LLMs.

faq.question: What are the limits of this skill?
faq.answer: It provides guidance and examples but no executable evaluation pipeline in this directory.

faq.question: How do I integrate with my stack?
faq.answer: Map the metrics and workflows to your existing evaluation or CI tools.

faq.question: Does it access or store my data?
faq.answer: No, it is static documentation and does not read or transmit data.

faq.question: What if scores are unstable?
faq.answer: Increase sample size, review variance, and add human validation before decisions.

faq.question: How is this different from a benchmark list?
faq.answer: It combines metrics, human review, and testing strategy rather than only listing benchmarks.
